title: quercus × townei
intro: Quercus × townei, also known as the hybrid chestnut oak, is a rare deciduous tree that is a natural hybrid of the white oak (Quercus alba) and the chestnut oak (Quercus montana). The bark and leaves of this tree have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus × townei are numerous, with its extracts having been traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Quercus × townei can be made by infusing the bark and leaves in water or oil, and can be used as teas, tinctures, or salves to harness its medicinal properties.
